descriptions.description:
The image shows a web browser (Chrome) running the p5.js Web Editor, displaying an HTML file and its corresponding visual output.

**Code (HTML):**
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/p5.js/1.11.1/p5.min.js"></script>
<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/p5.js/1.11.1/addons/sound.min.js"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="style.css">
<meta charset="utf-8" />
</head>
<body>
<main></main>
<script src="sketch.js"></script>
</body>
</html>
```

**Visual output:**
The preview pane on the right displays a canvas with a gradient background transitioning from green at the top to pink/magenta at the bottom. Overlaid on this gradient are numerous black and light gray circles of varying sizes, scattered in a seemingly random pattern.

**UI state:**
The application is the p5.js Web Editor, with `index.html` as the active tab in the file explorer on the left. The address bar shows a URL pointing to `editor.p5js.org`. A console area is visible at the bottom left, currently empty.

The screen shows a web browser displaying the MDN Web Docs documentation page for `HTMLCanvasElement.getContext()`.

**Code:**
```javascript
// Syntax section
getContext(contextType)
getContext(contextType, contextAttributes)

// Parameters section
"2d"
```

**Visual output:**
The main content area displays technical documentation including "Syntax," "Parameters," and a sidebar titled "In this article." A small advertisement for "BugHerd" is visible in the bottom right corner.

**UI state:**
The application is Google Chrome running on macOS. The active tab is "New Tab" (though the URL bar shows a Mozilla developer documentation page). The browser window includes standard macOS menu bars and window controls.

The image shows a web browser displaying the MDN Web Docs page for the `CanvasRenderingContext2D.createRadialGradient()` method.

**UI state:**
The browser is running Google Chrome on macOS, showing a tab for "developer.mozilla.org" with the URL `en-US/docs/Web/API/CanvasRenderingContext2D/createRadialGradient`. The page layout includes a sidebar with navigation links (e.g., "arc()", "beginPath()"), a main content area with the method title and syntax, and an "In this article" navigation menu on the right.

**Code:**
No code editor is visible, only documentation text and a syntax header:
```javascript
Syntax

JS
```

This screen shows a web browser displaying the MDN Web Docs documentation for the `CanvasRenderingContext2D.createRadialGradient()` method.

**UI state:**
The browser is running Google Chrome on macOS, displaying a page from `developer.mozilla.org`. The sidebar on the left shows a navigation menu for "Web APIs" with various canvas methods listed, including `arc()`, `clip()`, and the currently selected `createRadialGradient()`. The main content area displays documentation for the method, including a "Syntax" section.

**Code:**
The following code snippet is visible under the Syntax heading:

```javascript
// JS
ctx.createRadialGradient(x0, y0, r0, x1, y1, r1)
```

This screen shows a web browser displaying the MDN Web Docs documentation for the `createRadialGradient()` method.

**Code:**
```javascript
createRadialGradient(x0, y0, r0, x1, y1, r1)
```

**Visual output:**
The main content area displays the syntax and parameter definitions for the `createRadialGradient()` method. A sidebar on the left lists various canvas API methods, with `createRadialGradient()` highlighted in a dark blue box. A "In this article" navigation menu is visible on the right side of the page.

**UI state:**
The application is a Google Chrome web browser viewing `developer.mozilla.org`. The URL bar shows the path `/en-US/docs/Web/API/CanvasRenderingContext2D/createRadialGradient`. A small advertisement for "SurveyJS" is visible in the bottom right corner.

This screen shows the documentation page for the `beginClip()` function on the p5.js reference website.

**Code:**
```javascript
function setup() {
  createCanvas(100, 100);
}
```

**Visual output:**
The page displays a technical explanation of the `beginClip()` function, including its purpose and parameters. Below the text is an "Examples" section containing a code snippet in JavaScript. A yellow banner at the bottom of the screen contains the text, "Looking for the old p5.js site? Find it here!"

**UI state:**
The application is a Google Chrome web browser displaying the `p5js.org` website. The active tab is titled "beginClip". Navigation menus for English, Accessibility, and Search are visible at the top of the main content area.
